历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plans
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum ranking for Physics Category (including Science) was approximately 17972 → 30236 → 34599 → 32050 → 36322, showing a continuous backward trend, with a cumulative backward shift of about 18350 places over the past 4 years; the 2025 baseline is about 36322, an increase of about 4272 places compared to 2024, indicating a significant backward shift; the provincial Physics Category enrollment plan was 14 in 2021 → 26 in 2022 → 65 in 2023 → 65 in 2024 → 82 in 2025, with continuous expansion; the 2026 plan is 84, a slight increase of 2 (+2.4%) compared to 2025, with limited expansion impact on competition.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: 211 Project, Double First-Class construction university; Soft Science main ranking in 2026 is about 137th, with recent rankings fluctuating between 128 and 170, overall at a mid-to-lower level among 211 universities; 2026 reputation evaluation is neutral/mixed, mainly involving a library management incident, with no major negative public opinion; Douyin popularity ranking is 104th, rated as 'average', with low short video exposure and stable application attention; fifth round of discipline evaluation.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: Social popularity for engineering majors such as Computer Science, Electronic Information/Communication, and Electrical Engineering remains high, matching the university's offerings in Computer Science and Electrical Engineering, providing some support for related major applications; the total number of regular college entrance exam takers in the province in 2026 is about 167,000, a decrease of about 3% from 172,114 in 2025, slightly easing competition; the provincial control line for Physics Category undergraduate batch in 2025 is 360 points, and the special type enrollment control line is 472 points, unchanged from 2024, with basically stable exam difficulty.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the continuous backward trend of historical rankings, the slight expansion of the 2026 plan (+2.4%), and the decrease in the number of provincial examinees by about 3%, the expansion and examinee reduction effects partially offset each other; based on the 2025 baseline of 36322, the estimated minimum ranking for 2026 is about 36884, a continued slight backward shift of about 562 places from 2025, with a range of 36384–37384.
